idea-plugin.name: Codeup
idea-plugin.description:
Surfaces anti-patterns in your codebase using the Anthropic Claude API. Findings are persisted as YAML files under .codeup/ so they travel with the repo and go through PR review. The .codeup/ format is compatible with the Codeup VS Code extension — teams on mixed editors share a single findings directory.

What it detects

107 catalogued patterns spanning:

  • Single-file smells — god class, anemic domain model, long methods, primitive obsession, deep nesting, high cognitive complexity, error swallowing, function-name mismatches.
  • Inheritance & OO shape — non-exclusive subtypes, procedural shell classes (Manager/Handler/Processor), base classes that depend on subclasses, parallel inheritance hierarchies.
  • Cross-file structure — type leakage across boundaries, shotgun surgery, feature envy with neighbour context.
  • Module dependency — deterministic cyclic-dependency detection (Tarjan SCC), layer-boundary violations driven by an optional .codeup/intent.yaml.
  • Service-level — distributed monolith indicators, shared-database anti-patterns, reach-through reads, god service.
  • Data / persistence — N+1 queries, lost updates, cache-as-source-of-truth, EAV overuse, god tables.
  • Exception handling — overbroad catches, lossy translation, exceptions as validation, log-and-rethrow cargo cult.
  • Code security (10 patterns) — untrusted input in interpreting contexts, path traversal, unsafe deserialization, inconsistent validation across ingress paths, credentials scoped to identity rather than destination, and more.
  • Process / judgement — premature optimisation/abstraction, copy-paste programming, golden hammer, lava flow.

Setup

  1. Install the plugin from the marketplace (or install the ZIP via Settings → Plugins → ⚙ → Install Plugin from Disk…).
  2. Set an Anthropic API key via Tools → Codeup → Set Anthropic API Key. The key is stored in the IDE's secure credential store (macOS Keychain / Windows DPAPI / Linux libsecret) — never written to disk or committed to your repo.
  3. Open a project and run Tools → Codeup → Run Full Scan. The first run shows a cost estimate before making any API calls.

An Anthropic API key is required. Get one at console.anthropic.com. The default model is claude-sonnet-4-6, configurable via Settings → Tools → Codeup.

Daily flow

  1. Run a scan — Tools → Codeup → Run Full Scan (whole project) or Scan Current File. A cost estimate is shown before any full scan.
  2. Triage findings in the Codeup tool window (right panel):
    • Click a finding to open the file at the right line.
    • Confirm — becomes a positive exemplar in .codeup/knowledge/exemplars.yaml.
    • Dismiss… — prompts for a rationale saved to .codeup/knowledge/dismissals.yaml; future scans learn from it.
    • Mark Fixed once the change is made.
  3. Rescan — unchanged files come from cache (no API cost).

Commands (Tools → Codeup)

Run Full ScanScan every supported file. Shows cost estimate first.
Scan Current FileScan the active editor file only.
Scan Open TabsScan all currently open editor tabs.
Suggest Architectural IntentDraft a .codeup/intent.yaml from your project layout.
Set / Clear Anthropic API KeyManage the API key in the IDE credential store.
Check for UpdatesCheck GitHub Releases for a newer version (also auto-runs on startup).

Settings (Settings → Tools → Codeup)

ModelAnthropic model used for analysis (default: claude-sonnet-4-6).
Scan on saveRun an incremental scan on file save (default: off).
Findings directoryWhere findings YAML files live (default: .codeup/findings).
File size warn / critical (bytes)Thresholds for oversized-file findings (30 000 / 60 000).
Enable update checksPoll GitHub Releases for new versions on startup.

Data handling

Codeup sends source code to the Anthropic API. If you point Codeup at a client repository, source from that repository will leave your machine. Confirm this is acceptable under any NDA or data-handling agreement that applies.

What gets sent (over HTTPS to api.anthropic.com): the full text of each analysed file, up to 6 neighbour files (truncated to 8 000 chars each), dismissal rationales and exemplars from .codeup/knowledge/, and catalogue pattern hints.

What is never sent: files excluded by .gitignore or .codeupignore, files over 512 KB, unchanged files (cache hit), deterministic findings (cycles, layer violations), or your API key as content.

Anthropic's commercial API terms: inputs and outputs are not used to train models. Zero Data Retention agreements and AWS Bedrock / GCP Vertex deployments are available for stricter requirements.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.3

  • Suggest Architectural Intent — completely reworked: Claude now receives a structured project summary (top directories by file count + most-frequent cross-directory imports) and returns layer rules via a typed tool call instead of free-text YAML. Result is a clean, commented intent.yaml; falls back to heuristic default if the model call fails.
  • HTTP timeouts added to the Anthropic client (30 s connect/write, 120 s read) — prevents requests from hanging indefinitely on slow or dropped connections.
  • Fixed a .gitignore matching bug where plain-name patterns (e.g. .git) did not match directory paths that ended with a trailing slash.

1.0.2

  • Inline finding detail panel embedded in a split pane below the findings tree — details visible on selection without a separate view.
  • Status-aware action buttons: available actions update as a finding moves through Unconfirmed → Confirmed → Dismissed / Fixed, with a Reopen action on all terminal states.
  • History table in the detail panel showing every status transition with timestamp and note.
  • Priority and confidence now shown alongside severity in the detail view.
  • Direct Open Code link navigates to the exact file and line of a finding.
  • Explanation and remediation text rendered with paragraph breaks.

1.0.1

  • Initial Marketplace release.
  • 107 catalogued anti-patterns across single-file, OO shape, cross-file, module dependency, service-level, data/persistence, exception handling, security, and process categories.
  • Findings persisted as YAML under .codeup/ — compatible with the Codeup VS Code extension.
  • Triage workflow: Confirm, Dismiss (with rationale), Mark Fixed.
  • Incremental scanning with file-level cache (no API cost for unchanged files).
  • Cost estimate shown before any full-project scan.

action.description: Scan all files in the project for architectural anti-patterns
Scan all currently open editor tabs
Draft a .codeup/intent.yaml based on your project structure
Store the Anthropic API key in the system credential store
Remove the stored Anthropic API key
Check GitHub Releases for a newer version of Codeup
